title: china / electric vehicles / lithium batteries | Economic Commentary | China's Electric Trade
summary: This blog post examines China's trade in electric vehicles, lithium batteries, and solar panels, finding that plug-in car and battery exports contributed $65 billion to China's $270 billion increase in trade surplus from 2022 to 2025. China supplied 70% of U.S. lithium-ion battery imports and 86% of the EU's in 2024, while Chinese solar panel export revenues fell 40% despite higher volumes due to a supply glut. The analysis suggests China's dominance in electric technology trade is likely to persist near-term.
